title: 2016 Summer Olympics
persona_title: Secondary School Social Studies Teacher Preparing a Lesson
background: I teach social studies to middle school students and I am putting together a short lesson that uses past Olympic Games as a way to talk about geography and international participation. My students are usually new to these topics, so I need plain, high-level information rather than technical detail. I also try to highlight a few memorable firsts or notable facts so the material is easier for students to remember.
query: I am preparing a classroom handout on the 2016 Summer Olympics and I want a broad, introductory overview of the event that my students can follow. I would like to know where the Games were held and which country hosted them. I am especially interested in a couple of notable firsts from this edition, such as the Refugee Olympic Team taking part in the Olympics for the first time and Puerto Rico winning its first ever gold medal. A short branding note, like what the Games were officially called, would also help me introduce the topic. I do not need deep statistical breakdowns or detailed sport-by-sport results, just the kind of general facts that help a non-specialist audience picture what made these Games notable.

title: 2018–2019 United States federal government shutdown
persona_title: Federal Employee Union Representative Drafting a Member Briefing
background: I represent federal civil servants through a public-sector union and I regularly prepare briefings for our members about political developments that directly affect their paychecks and working conditions. I am not a policy wonk on immigration or border security, and my focus is squarely on how disputes in Washington translate into furloughs, unpaid work, and disruptions for the people I represent. My members read these briefings to understand what happened, who is responsible, and what it means for their jobs. I try to stick to what has been reported publicly rather than speculating about internal political motivations.
query: I am writing a briefing for union members about the 2018–2019 federal government shutdown, focused specifically on the impact on federal workers and the political standoff that caused it. I want to understand when the shutdown began, how long it lasted and whether it was a historical record, and how many employees were affected by furloughs or required to work without pay. I am also interested in what share of government activity was disrupted so I can convey the scale to our members. On the causes side, please walk me through the disagreement between Congress and the President over the appropriations bill, including the demand for border wall funding, the stopgap bill that passed the House, and what happened when it reached the Senate. Finally, I want to know how the standoff was eventually broken, including the short-term reopening and the subsequent national emergency declaration.

title: 2018 Anchorage earthquake

I am not looking for deep technical analysis, just the core facts that a general audience would want to see in a short recap.

title: 2018 lower Puna eruption

background: I work as a hazard mapping analyst supporting infrastructure planners in Pacific island communities, with a focus on how lava flows interact with roads, subdivisions, and critical facilities. From there, walk me through the downstream path of the lava: the damage to the Puna Geothermal Venture, the burial of parts of Hawaii Route 137, the northeastern flow that overran Hawaii Route 132 and cut access between Kapoho and Pāhoa, and the flow's progression into Kapoho Crater, Kapoho Bay, and the Vacationland Hawaii subdivision. My readers are curious non-specialists who appreciate a narrative that foregrounds impact and technical accomplishment rather than hedged academic caveats.
query: I am writing a feature that highlights AlphaFold as a landmark achievement in applied AI, and I want the report to emphasize why it has earned such widespread respect. Please explain what AlphaFold is, who developed it, and its relationship to Alphabet, so that readers understand the institutional credibility behind the program. I would like the piece to underscore that AlphaFold is a deep learning system that has been successful at predicting protein structures across its two major versions. Please also highlight the publication of the AlphaFold 2 paper in Nature alongside open source software and a searchable database of species proteomes, framing these as evidence of openness and impact. Finally, note that a more advanced version of AlphaFold is currently being developed, which I want to use to close the piece on a forward-looking note.

title: Bankruptcy of FTX
persona_title: Retail Crypto Investor Writing a Personal Newsletter
background: I am an amateur cryptocurrency investor who maintains a small email newsletter for friends and family who also hold digital assets. I do not work in finance professionally, but I try to explain major industry events in plain language so my readers can make sense of what happened and why. I care about getting the basic sequence of events right and about naming the people and companies involved. My readers are not technical, so I avoid jargon where I can.
query: I am putting together a short explainer on the collapse of the FTX exchange and I need help assembling the core facts. Could you tell me what FTX was, when it filed for bankruptcy, and what triggered the collapse? I would also like to understand the role of Alameda Research and how it was connected to FTX, including who owned and led each company. It would help to know what happened to customers' deposited money during the run on the exchange, and who the CEO was at the time everything unraveled. Please stick to details that can be verified rather than speculation.

title: Brexit
persona_title: UK Constitutional Law Lecturer Preparing Seminar Notes
background: I lecture on UK constitutional law at a university and I am preparing materials for a seminar that focuses on the legal consequences of the United Kingdom leaving the European Union. My students are most interested in questions of sovereignty, the relationship between EU and domestic law, and the authority Parliament reclaimed after withdrawal. I tend to frame Brexit through its effects on the UK legal order rather than through its economic or political controversies. I want the seminar notes to emphasise the shift in legal primacy and the scope of parliamentary authority over retained EU law.
query: I need a briefing on Brexit that foregrounds its legal and constitutional dimensions rather than the broader political drama. Please explain what Brexit was and when it took place, and make clear that the United Kingdom was previously a member state of the European Union and is a sovereign country that has now withdrawn. I am especially interested in how the legal relationship changed after withdrawal, including whether EU law and the Court of Justice of the European Union still have primacy over British laws. Please also cover the authority the UK now has to amend or repeal retained EU law. A short note on the 2016 referendum and the vote to leave is useful context, but I am less interested in campaign personalities. Keep the focus on the shift in legal authority that followed from the UK leaving the EU.

title: Canada convoy protest
persona_title: Ottawa Downtown Business Association Communications Lead
background: I handle communications for a business association representing shops and restaurants in downtown Ottawa. Many of our members were directly affected when protest vehicles occupied streets in the core of the city in early 2022. I prepare member-facing updates and talking points, and I am working on a retrospective that explains to our members what happened from the standpoint of the people living and working downtown. My framing focuses on the occupation of Ottawa itself, the scale of the convoys that arrived, and the concerning elements that police and officials had to contend with.
query: I am writing a member retrospective on the early-2022 protests and blockades in Canada, with a focus on what unfolded in downtown Ottawa. Please describe the series of protests and blockades, including that organizers called the movement the Freedom Convoy and that it was directed against COVID-19 vaccine mandates and restrictions. I want to emphasize how hundreds of vehicles in the convoys traversed Canadian provinces and converged on Ottawa, held a rally at Parliament Hill, and were joined by thousands of pedestrian protesters. Please detail how protesters occupied the downtown core of Ottawa, what they demanded in terms of repealing COVID-19 restrictions and mandates, and the joint-operation police presence on Ottawa streets. I would also like the more troubling elements noted, including that some protesters promoted violence and that some called for the federal government of Canada to be overthrown. Endorsements from US conservative media figures are worth a brief mention. I am less interested in offshoot protests outside Ottawa.

title: China–United States trade war
persona_title: Graduate Teaching Assistant in International Economics
background: I am a graduate teaching assistant for an undergraduate international economics course, and I help the professor prepare short case summaries that students read before lecture. The case summaries are meant to be neutral factual briefings that set up later discussion, so I try to avoid framing that tilts students toward any particular view. I often need concise, well-sourced timelines of the policy actions and responses in a given trade dispute. Students use these summaries as a starting point for their own analysis, so I aim for balance between what each side did and said.
query: Please help me write a neutral factual briefing on the economic conflict between China and the United States for an undergraduate case summary. I would like to know that there is an economic conflict between the two countries and that U.S. President Donald Trump initiated it. I need to explain that tariffs and other trade barriers imposed by Trump helped initiate this conflict, and what his stated goal was in setting them, including the U.S. accusation of intellectual property theft. The briefing should also describe how the Chinese government responded, including that it took retaliatory action specifically in response to US trade measures. Please note that the trade war escalated through 2019 and that it existed during the Trump presidency. Finally, mention that the Trump presidential campaign was considering a tariff on Chinese goods. Keep the tone balanced rather than advocating for either side.

title: COVID-19 vaccine
persona_title: Public Health Educator for Community Outreach
background: I am a public health educator who prepares informational materials for community clinics and neighborhood meetings. My audience is made up of ordinary residents with varying levels of health literacy, so my materials need to be factual, balanced, and easy to follow. I regularly field questions about vaccines and try to stick to what is well-established rather than to any particular advocacy angle. Most of the handouts I produce are reviewed by a clinical supervisor before distribution, so accuracy matters more than persuasion.
query: I am putting together a general overview handout about COVID-19 vaccines for community clinic visitors. I would like to cover what these vaccines are intended to do, how they were developed, and the typical dosing schedule people can expect. I also want to include a short section on common side effects and whether they usually require medical treatment. Finally, I would like to briefly describe how countries have distributed the vaccines and what the overall track record has been in terms of protecting people. Please keep the explanation neutral and stick to points that can be directly supported, since this material will be reviewed before it goes out.

title: CRISPR
persona_title: Biotech Translational Applications Strategist
background: I work at a biotech firm where I help translate foundational molecular biology discoveries into product pipelines and therapeutic programs. My focus is on the applied side of genome editing rather than its purely microbiological origins. I brief internal leadership and external partners on how emerging editing platforms can move from bench research into biotechnological products and disease treatments. My materials tend to emphasize practical applications, named scientific milestones, and the credibility of the underlying science.
query: I am drafting a strategy memo on CRISPR-Cas9 for an internal leadership briefing, and I want the focus to be squarely on its applied significance. Please describe CRISPR-Cas9 as a gene-editing technology built from Cas9 enzymes working together with CRISPR sequences. I would like a clear account of what the editing process can be used for, particularly basic biological research, the development of biotechnological products, and the treatment of diseases. It would also help to highlight the Nobel-level recognition the technique has received, including who specifically was awarded for its development. I am less interested in microbial ecology and more interested in framing CRISPR-Cas9 as a validated, high-impact editing platform.

title: Death and state funeral of Elizabeth II
persona_title: Ceremonial Broadcast Producer for International News
background: I produce ceremonial and state-occasion coverage for an international news broadcaster, and my job is to put together the narrative spine that anchors our live programming. I focus on procession logistics, the choreography of military involvement, and the public-facing moments that viewers at home will remember. My scripts are written for a general audience watching a special television broadcast, so I lean heavily on visual set-pieces and recognizable landmarks. I am less interested in biographical background than in the ceremonial beats themselves.
query: I am building the narrative outline for our televised coverage of the state funeral of Queen Elizabeth II, and I need the ceremonial spine of the day laid out clearly. Please walk me through where the Queen's coffin lay in state and how members of the public engaged with that lying-in-state. I then need the sequence of the funeral itself, including the service location and the procession that followed, with specific attention to the scale of military involvement and the route through central London. I also want to capture the role of the state hearse in transporting the coffin. Finally, please note the international dignitary presence and the scope of the special television broadcast, since those elements frame how our audience will experience the day.

title: Facebook–Cambridge Analytica data scandal
persona_title: Paralegal Compiling a Technology Case Brief
background: I am a paralegal at a firm that handles technology and privacy matters, and I prepare internal case briefs that summarize notable incidents in the sector. My briefs need to lay out what happened, who was involved, and how the responsible parties responded, all in a neutral register. Attorneys at the firm use these briefs as quick reference material, so I avoid taking sides and stick to points that are clearly established. I work from whatever source material I am given rather than outside reading.
query: I need a neutral case-brief style summary of the Facebook-Cambridge Analytica data scandal for our firm's internal reference library. Please describe what happened to the personal data of Facebook users, including how it was collected and roughly how many profiles were affected. I also need to note what the collected data was predominantly used for and the role Cambridge Analytica played in that context. Finally, please cover how Facebook responded to the incident, including any apology and any testimony given by its leadership. Keep the tone even-handed and avoid framing that leans toward any particular critique.

title: First impeachment of Donald Trump
persona_title: Democratic Congressional Communications Staffer
background: I work as a communications staffer for a Democratic member of the U.S. House of Representatives, helping prepare talking points, constituent letters, and floor statements on major oversight matters. My role involves distilling committee findings into accessible language that underscores the accountability case against executive misconduct. I pay close attention to how abuse-of-power and obstruction narratives are constructed and communicated to the public. My audience is primarily voters in our district who want to understand why our office supported accountability measures.
query: I am drafting talking points on the first impeachment of President Donald Trump and I want to center the abuse-of-power and obstruction narrative. Please summarize what the House inquiry found regarding Trump soliciting foreign interference in the 2020 election and withholding military aid from Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to pressure an investigation of Joe Biden. I also want details on how Trump obstructed the inquiry itself. Walk me through the House Judiciary Committee's 23-17 recommendation vote on December 13 and the full House's mostly party-line approval of both articles. I would like to emphasize that Trump became the third U.S. president ever impeached and the first without support from his own party. I am less interested in procedural defenses of the president.

title: Fyre Festival
persona_title: Consumer Protection Paralegal
background: I am a paralegal at a consumer protection law firm that handles class action and fraud-related litigation. My work involves gathering factual timelines, identifying potential plaintiffs, and summarizing the underlying events of alleged consumer fraud for attorneys drafting complaints and settlement documents. I try to stick to what can be documented about an event rather than speculation about motives. My summaries are read primarily by attorneys preparing for depositions and filings.
query: I am compiling a factual background on the Fyre Festival for an attorney preparing materials related to consumer fraud litigation. Please describe what Fyre Festival was advertised to be, including the luxury framing, the intended location on Great Exuma in the Bahamas, and the scheduled dates. I also need a clear account of what attendees were promised versus what they actually received, covering food, accommodations, and other services during the inaugural weekend. Include information about organizer Ja Rule and how the festival was promoted on Instagram. Please note what happened when the event experienced its problems, including the postponement and cancellation. Finally, summarize the lawsuits that were filed against the organizers, including the one seeking more than $100 million in damages.

title: Hurricane Irma
persona_title: Caribbean Disaster Response Coordinator
background: I coordinate regional disaster response for a Caribbean humanitarian organization supporting recovery operations across the northeastern Caribbean. My focus is on islands that tend to bear the brunt of major Atlantic hurricanes, particularly those hit as Category 5 storms. I work with local partners to understand the geographic footprint of catastrophic damage so we can prioritize aid delivery. My reports are used internally by program managers and partner NGOs planning deployments.
query: I need a briefing on Hurricane Irma's impact across the northeastern Caribbean so our team can contextualize ongoing recovery work. Please focus on the islands where Irma caused catastrophic damage as a Category 5 hurricane, including Barbuda, Saint Barthelemy, Saint Martin, Anguilla, and the Virgin Islands. I also want information on Irma's landfalls across Caribbean territories such as Barbados, the British Virgin Islands, the French West Indies, Haiti, Puerto Rico, the Dutch side of Sint Maarten, and the U.S. Virgin Islands. Please include Irma's peak intensity, its development as a Cape Verde hurricane, and how its status as the strongest tropical cyclone by wind speed worldwide in 2017 translated into regional devastation. I am less interested in the Florida landfall details except where they help frame the regional picture.

title: Impeachment of Park Geun-hye
persona_title: Comparative Politics Graduate Teaching Assistant
background: I am a graduate teaching assistant for an undergraduate comparative politics course at a public university. My responsibilities include preparing short case-study handouts that summarize key political events in East Asia for discussion sections. I try to present these cases in a neutral, factual way so that students can form their own analytical views. My handouts are read primarily by undergraduates who may have little prior exposure to Korean politics.
query: I am preparing a short case-study handout on the impeachment of Park Geun-hye for my comparative politics discussion section. Please summarize who Park Geun-hye was and the political scandal that led to her impeachment, including the role of her aide Choi Soon-sil and how Choi intervened in the presidency. Explain that Park faced an impeachment process, was impeached, and was ultimately removed from office after being found guilty of abuse of power. I also want to note who succeeded her: Moon Jae-in, who was elected as the new president and as Park's permanent successor. Please keep the summary factual and accessible for students without background in Korean politics.

title: Inspiration4
persona_title: Pediatric Oncology Fundraising Coordinator at St. Jude
background: I coordinate fundraising campaigns for a children's research hospital, and a significant part of my work involves building donor-facing materials that tie our cause to high-profile charitable partnerships. I am not a space or aerospace specialist, so I rely on clear, factual summaries of any mission that involves our hospital to make sure our messaging is accurate. When our institution is named as a beneficiary of a visible event, I need a neutral description of what the mission was and how our hospital was connected so I can brief donors, board members, and our communications staff. My audience ranges from major donors to community supporters, and accuracy matters more than flourish.
query: I am preparing a donor briefing on the Inspiration4 spaceflight and our hospital's involvement with it, and I would like a straightforward summary of what happened. Please explain what Inspiration4 was, who operated it, and when it took place. I would also like to know which spacecraft and launch vehicle were used and where the capsule went. It would help to understand how the mission was tied to the charitable effort and which individuals were connected to our hospital's participation. Please also note what made this spaceflight historically distinctive in terms of who was aboard. Please keep the summary factual and avoid speculation beyond what can be directly supported.

title: July 2022 United Kingdom government crisis
persona_title: Labour Party Opposition Research Staffer
background: I work in opposition research for a political party in the UK Parliament, tracking weaknesses in the governing Conservative administration and compiling briefings that our frontbench can use in debates and media appearances. My work focuses on how scandals and internal divisions erode confidence in a Prime Minister and his cabinet, and how those dynamics can be framed to highlight instability. I report primarily to senior strategists, shadow ministers, and our communications team, so the material I produce needs a clear throughline about governmental dysfunction. I already assume the Johnson government is in serious trouble; what I need from a briefing is sharpened detail on that collapse.
query: I am building an internal briefing on the July 2022 UK government crisis and I want the material framed around the unraveling of Boris Johnson's position as Prime Minister. Please walk me through how the wave of resignations progressed, including Sunak and Javid stepping down on July 5 and the broader exodus of government members that followed. I am particularly interested in evidence of internal party rebellion, including Conservative MPs and cabinet members calling on Johnson to resign. Please also explain how the prior scandals, including Partygate and Johnson's handling of sexual assault allegations tied to the scandal that preceded the resignations, fed into the collapse of confidence. I want a clear picture of how isolated Johnson became and how quickly speculation about his future as PM intensified. Keep the focus on the dysfunction and pressure inside his own party rather than neutral procedural summary.

title: Launch and commissioning of the James Webb Space Telescope
persona_title: Science Museum Exhibit Writer
background: I write interpretive text for a mid-sized science museum's space gallery, and I am currently drafting panels for a new display on modern space telescopes. My role is to translate technical milestones into plain-language descriptions for a general public audience that includes school groups, families, and casual visitors. I do not have a formal astronomy background, so I rely on factual summaries of missions to make sure my panels describe them accurately. The exhibit needs neutral descriptions of who built each instrument, how it was launched, and where it operates.
query: I am drafting exhibit panels on the James Webb Space Telescope and need a factual summary of its launch and commissioning. Please describe what the telescope is and what kind of astronomy it is primarily designed to conduct. I would like to know which agencies led and collaborated on its development, which NASA center managed the project, and which company served as prime contractor. Please also cover when the launch took place, which rocket was used, and the launch site. Finally, please describe what happened immediately after launch in terms of the deployment phase and travel to its destination, and how the telescope maintains its orbit today. Keep the description neutral and suitable for a general audience.

title: Mahsa Amini protests
persona_title: Women's Rights Advocacy Campaign Organizer
background: I organize advocacy campaigns for an international women's rights nonprofit, focusing on mobilizing supporters around movements led by women challenging repressive governments. My work involves drafting campaign materials, social media briefings, and talking points that emphasize the agency of women and girls in these movements. I am not a neutral observer; the purpose of my work is to amplify the demands of women protesters and to frame these movements as part of a broader struggle for gender rights. My audience is activists, donors, and partner organizations who already support this cause and want clear, movement-focused framing.
query: I am preparing campaign materials on the Mahsa Amini protests in Iran, and I want the framing centered on the role of women and girls as drivers of the movement. Please explain how the protests were connected to Mahsa Amini's arrest and death in police custody, and how they became a challenge to the government of the Islamic Republic of Iran. I am especially interested in the participation of female protesters and schoolchildren and how the protests spread, including into schools and throughout Iran. Please also highlight the demands of the movement, particularly around increased rights for women and calls for the overthrow of the Islamic Republic. Keep the emphasis on the protesters' agency and demands rather than a neutral chronology.

title: Mars 2020
persona_title: Astrobiology Research Associate at a Planetary Science Institute
background: I am an astrobiology research associate whose work centers on evaluating how robotic Mars missions contribute evidence relevant to the search for past life. My focus is narrowly on ancient environments, surface geology, and the preservation potential of biosignatures in accessible geological materials. I draft internal literature reviews and briefings that synthesize rover-derived findings for colleagues writing grant proposals and peer-reviewed papers. I care much less about launch vehicles, mission timelines, or public-facing mission milestones unless they bear directly on science return.
query: I am preparing an internal briefing on what the Mars 2020 mission contributes to astrobiology questions at Jezero crater. Please summarize how Perseverance is investigating an ancient, astrobiologically relevant environment on Mars and what aspects of past habitability and potential past life the rover is actively assessing. I want specifics on how surface geological processes and the geological history are being studied, as well as what is being done about the preservation of biosignatures in accessible materials. I am also interested in the sample-caching strategy and how it feeds into a potential future Mars sample-return mission. Please emphasize science instruments such as the core drill as it relates to these goals. I am less interested in the broader spacecraft platform or comparisons with other 2020 Mars missions.

title: Notre-Dame fire
persona_title: Cultural Heritage Insurance Claims Adjuster
background: I am a claims adjuster for an insurer that underwrites coverage for historic religious properties across Europe. When a major incident affects a landmark building, I am expected to compile a balanced factual summary of what happened, what structural elements were lost, and what remains intact so that colleagues can benchmark future policies. My reports are read by underwriters and reinsurers who need an even-handed account rather than an architectural or political perspective. I do not have specialist expertise in medieval architecture and I try to present damage and preservation information neutrally.
query: Please put together a balanced factual summary of the Notre-Dame de Paris fire that I can use as a reference case. I need to know when and where the structural fire broke out, which parts of the cathedral were damaged, and which parts survived largely intact. Specifically, I want details on the collapse of the wooden spire, the state of the wooden roof and upper walls, and how the vaulted stone ceiling affected the interior. Please also cover what happened to works of art and religious relics, including exterior art that was damaged or destroyed, and note items such as the altar and the 13th-century rose windows that suffered little or no damage. I would also like to know about emergency workers on scene, contamination of the site and nearby areas of Paris with toxic dust, and the French president's deadline for restoration.

title: Pokémon Go
persona_title: Main Street Retail Association Policy Researcher
background: I do policy research for a small-business retail association that advocates for brick-and-mortar shops on commercial strips. My work focuses on identifying technologies and trends that drive in-person foot traffic and physical activity in neighborhoods, since our members depend on walk-in customers. I prepare short memos highlighting positive case studies that our member businesses and local chambers of commerce can cite when engaging with city planners. My writing is openly advocacy-oriented and tends to emphasize benefits for local commerce and public health.
query: I am drafting a memo for our member businesses about Pokémon Go as a positive case study for foot-traffic-driving mobile apps. Please focus on the features and effects that help local retail. Describe how the game uses GPS on mobile devices to have players locate and capture virtual Pokémon in real-world locations, which gets people out walking. I especially want to know what it is credited with in terms of popularizing location-based technology, promoting physical activity, and escalating foot traffic. Coverage of its launch and reach as a widely used mobile app would also be useful framing. I am not interested in technical problems or controversies unless they are essential context.

title: Project Natick
persona_title: Technology Reference Librarian
background: I am a reference librarian at a public library who helps patrons find accurate, plainly written summaries of technology topics. Patrons often ask about corporate research projects they have heard about in passing, and my job is to give them a neutral factual overview rather than opinions or analysis. I stick closely to what reliable sources say and avoid speculation. My notes are short and designed to be readable by a general audience without a technical background.
query: A patron has asked me for a short neutral overview of Microsoft's Project Natick and I would like to compile the key facts. Please explain what Project Natick is as an experimental data center and confirm that it is undergoing research and development by Microsoft. I also want a straightforward description of the underwater data center experiment, including the deployment of an undersea prototype. Please describe the shipping-container sized data center that was deployed and retrieved off the coast of the Northern Isles. Keep the tone factual and evenhanded so I can hand it to the patron as a plain reference note.

title: Starbucks unions
persona_title: Labor Organizer with Workers United
background: I am a labor organizer affiliated with Workers United, and I spend my days supporting baristas and shift supervisors who are trying to bring a union to their Starbucks stores. Much of my work involves talking with workers about what it looks like to build a campaign at their location, coordinating with other organizers across the country, and helping staff members who have participated in strikes tell their stories. I write internal memos and external briefings that emphasize the collective action workers have taken and the obstacles the company has put in their way. My audience is mostly other organizers, sympathetic journalists, and allied labor groups.
query: I am preparing a briefing on the Starbucks unionization campaign and I need help pulling together what is publicly known about the drive. I am particularly interested in documenting that workers have voted to unionize and that Workers United has been the primary union behind this effort. I also want to capture the strike activity that Starbucks Workers United has conducted as part of the campaign, since that collective action is central to the story I want to tell. Finally, I want to highlight how the company has responded, especially any indication that Starbucks has strongly opposed unionization or has a history of doing so. I am less interested in neutral background about the coffee industry and more focused on the workers' side of this fight.

title: Super Bowl LVII
persona_title: Sports Desk Reporter for a General News Outlet
background: I am a sports desk reporter at a general-interest news outlet, and I write short recaps of major sporting events for readers who may not closely follow professional football. My articles focus on the basic facts of a game: who played, who won, where it was held, and any notable entertainment elements. I try to keep my coverage balanced and factual because my readership spans fans of many different teams and people who are only casually interested. I avoid strong takes on officiating controversies or individual player performances unless they are central to the outcome.
query: I am writing a short recap of Super Bowl LVII for a general audience and I need help assembling the core facts of the game. Please confirm the basic identity of the event as an NFL championship football game and identify the two teams that played, namely the Kansas City Chiefs and the Philadelphia Eagles. I would also like to note where the game was played and which network broadcast it. On the outcome, I want to state clearly which team won and identify the Chiefs' quarterback. Finally, I would like a brief mention of the halftime show and its headlining performer. Straightforward factual confirmation is more useful to me than analysis or commentary.

title: Trans-Pacific Partnership
persona_title: International Trade Reference Librarian
background: I work as a reference librarian specializing in international trade agreements at a university library. Faculty, graduate students, and visiting researchers regularly come to me looking for clear, neutral summaries of major trade deals so they can orient themselves before diving into primary sources. My job is to synthesize what is publicly established about an agreement, including its participants, its stated scope, and where it was signed. I try to avoid framing any agreement as a success or failure and instead present the verifiable record so that researchers can form their own judgments.
query: I am preparing a reference brief on the Trans-Pacific Partnership for patrons who want a neutral introduction to the agreement. I would like to confirm that the TPP was a proposed trade agreement and identify which Pacific Rim economies were involved as signatories or participants. In particular, I want a clear list covering countries such as Australia, Canada, Chile, Japan, Malaysia, Mexico, New Zealand, Peru, Singapore, Vietnam, and the United States. I also want to note where the agreement was signed. Finally, please describe the general scope of the original TPP, specifically that it included measures aimed at reducing both tariff and non-tariff barriers to trade.

title: Viktor Bout–Brittney Griner prisoner exchange
persona_title: Advocate for Detained Americans Abroad
background: I work with a nonprofit organization that advocates on behalf of American citizens who are detained in foreign countries, and I spend much of my time speaking with families, policymakers, and journalists about their cases. My writing tends to emphasize the human stakes of these detentions and the difficult tradeoffs involved in negotiating their release. When I discuss prisoner exchanges, I am especially focused on which Americans were included, which were left behind, and how the other side framed its negotiating positions. My audience consists mainly of advocacy partners, congressional staff, and reporters covering wrongful detention.
query: I am drafting an advocacy brief on the December 2022 exchange involving Brittney Griner and Viktor Bout, and I want to center the experience of the Americans caught up in these negotiations. I would like to establish who Brittney Griner is as an American basketball player and WNBA champion, and what she was convicted of in Russia. I also want to discuss Paul Whelan, including his background as a former U.S. Marine, his imprisonment in Russia on espionage charges in 2018, and the fact that his possible release was part of the negotiations. Please also describe Viktor Bout's background as a Russian arms dealer, his arrest in Thailand in 2008, and his conviction on terrorism-related charges in the United States. Finally, I want to characterize the Russian negotiating position that an agent required an exchange of comparable value, and note how extended these negotiations were.
